summary: White oleander contains an active toxin called nerin that causes severe cardiac issues; hence, it aligns with option \(\boxed{\text{B}}\) being accurate based on this information about its primary effect and composition. To ensure clarity while maintaining brevity, I will rephrase slightly but keep core details intact. Cardiotoxic compounds like those found from nerin are central to understanding why white oleander poses a serious health risk primarily via heart-related symptoms or damage. Option \(B\) accurately reflects these critical aspects related directly back to identifying specific toxins within plants causing harm predominantly by affecting cardiovascular function. This summary captures both essential elements succinctly without extraneous detail. Agent2 concludes their response emphasizing how the presence of cardiotonic glycosides such as digitalis-like substances derived from nerins impacts overall plant safety concerns linked closely enough
